title: The puzzle of motivation | Dan Pink | TED

transcript.chinese: 我首先需要在这里坦白。
transcript.english: I need to make a confession at the outset here.

transcript.chinese: 二十多年前，我做了一件让我后悔的事，
transcript.english: A little over 20 years ago, I did something that I regret,

transcript.chinese: 我并不感到特别自豪。
transcript.english: something that I'm not particularly proud of.

transcript.chinese: 从很多方面来说，我希望没有人知道这一点，但在这里我觉得有必要透露一下。
transcript.english: Something that, in many ways, I wish no one would ever know, but here I feel kind of obliged to reveal.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 20 世纪 80 年代末，在年轻时的轻率时刻，我进入了法学院。
transcript.english: (Laughter) In the late 1980s, in a moment of youthful indiscretion, I went to law school.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）在美国，法律是一个专业学位：获得大学学位后，你可以继续进入法学院。
transcript.english: (Laughter) In America, law is a professional degree: after your university degree, you go on to law school.

transcript.chinese: 当我进入法学院时，我的成绩并不好。
transcript.english: When I got to law school, I didn't do very well.

transcript.chinese: 温和地说，我做得不太好。
transcript.english: To put it mildly, I didn't do very well.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，我在法学院课程中毕业的那部分课程使前 90% 成为可能。
transcript.english: I, in fact, graduated in the part of my law school class that made the top 90% possible.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）谢谢。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Thank you.

transcript.chinese: 我一生中从未从事过法律工作。我几乎不被允许这样做。
transcript.english: I never practiced law a day in my life; I pretty much wasn't allowed to.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）但是今天，根据我更好的判断，根据我自己妻子的建议，我想尝试掸掉一些法律技能——那些法律技能中剩下的部分。
transcript.english: (Laughter) But today, against my better judgment, against the advice of my own wife, I want to try to dust off some of those legal skills -- what's left of those legal skills.

transcript.chinese: 我不想给你讲故事。
transcript.english: I don't want to tell you a story.

transcript.chinese: 我想做一个案例。
transcript.english: I want to make a case.

transcript.chinese: 我想提出一个冷静的、基于证据的、我敢说是律师的案例，以重新思考我们如何经营我们的业务。
transcript.english: I want to make a hard-headed, evidence-based, dare I say lawyerly case, for rethinking how we run our businesses.

transcript.chinese: 那么，陪审团的女士们先生们，请看一下这个。
transcript.english: So, ladies and gentlemen of the jury, take a look at this.

transcript.chinese: 这就是所谓的蜡烛问题。
transcript.english: This is called the candle problem.

transcript.chinese: 你们中有些人可能知道。
transcript.english: Some of you might know it.

transcript.chinese: 它是由一位名叫卡尔·邓克 (Karl Duncker) 的心理学家于 1945 年创建的。
transcript.english: It's created in 1945 by a psychologist named Karl Duncker.

transcript.chinese: 他创建的这个实验被用于行为科学的许多其他实验。
transcript.english: He created this experiment that is used in many other experiments in behavioral science.

transcript.chinese: 这是它的工作原理。
transcript.english: And here's how it works.

transcript.chinese: 假设我是实验者。
transcript.english: Suppose I'm the experimenter.

transcript.chinese: 我带你进一个房间。
transcript.english: I bring you into a room.

transcript.chinese: 我给你一支蜡烛、一些图钉和一些火柴。
transcript.english: I give you a candle, some thumbtacks and some matches.

transcript.chinese: 我对你说，“你的工作就是把蜡烛固定在墙上，这样蜡就不会滴到桌子上。”现在你会做什么？
transcript.english: And I say to you, "Your job is to attach the candle to the wall so the wax doesn't drip onto the table." Now what would you do?

transcript.chinese: 许多人开始尝试将蜡烛用图钉钉在墙上。
transcript.english: Many people begin trying to thumbtack the candle to the wall.

transcript.chinese: 不起作用。
transcript.english: Doesn't work.

transcript.chinese: 我看到有人在这里做出了一个动作——有些人有一个好主意，他们点燃火柴，融化蜡烛的一侧，尝试将它粘在墙上。
transcript.english: I saw somebody kind of make the motion over here -- some people have a great idea where they light the match, melt the side of the candle, try to adhere it to the wall.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个很棒的主意。
transcript.english: It's an awesome idea.

transcript.chinese: 不起作用。
transcript.english: Doesn't work.

transcript.chinese: 最终，五到十分钟后，大多数人都会找到解决方案，您可以在此处查看。
transcript.english: And eventually, after five or ten minutes, most people figure out the solution, which you can see here.

transcript.chinese: 关键是要克服所谓的功能固定性。
transcript.english: The key is to overcome what's called functional fixedness.

transcript.chinese: 你看那个盒子，你只会看到它是大头钉的容器。
transcript.english: You look at that box and you see it only as a receptacle for the tacks.

transcript.chinese: 但它还可以具有其他功能，作为蜡烛的平台。
transcript.english: But it can also have this other function, as a platform for the candle.

transcript.chinese: 蜡烛问题。
transcript.english: The candle problem.

transcript.chinese: 我想告诉你一个利用蜡烛问题的实验，是由一位名叫萨姆·格鲁克斯伯格（Sam Glucksberg）的科学家完成的，他现在在美国普林斯顿大学，这显示了激励的力量。
transcript.english: I want to tell you about an experiment using the candle problem, done by a scientist named Sam Glucksberg, who is now at Princeton University, US, This shows the power of incentives.

transcript.chinese: 他召集了参与者并说道：“我要给你们计时，看看你们能多快解决这个问题。”他对一组人说：“我将给你们计时，让你们建立规范，平均解决这类问题通常需要多长时间。”他向第二组提供了奖励。
transcript.english: He gathered his participants and said: "I'm going to time you, how quickly you can solve this problem." To one group he said, "I'm going to time you to establish norms, averages for how long it typically takes someone to solve this sort of problem." To the second group he offered rewards.

transcript.chinese: 他说，“如果你在最快时间中名列前 25%，你将获得 5 美元。
transcript.english: He said, "If you're in the top 25% of the fastest times, you get five dollars.

transcript.chinese: 如果你是我们今天测试的所有人中最快的，你将获得 20 美元。”现在这是几年前的事了，根据通货膨胀进行调整，对于几分钟的工作来说这是一笔不错的钱。
transcript.english: If you're the fastest of everyone we're testing here today, you get 20 dollars." Now this is several years ago, adjusted for inflation, it's a decent sum of money for a few minutes of work.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个很好的激励因素。
transcript.english: It's a nice motivator.

transcript.chinese: 问题：这个小组解决问题的速度有多快？
transcript.english: Question: How much faster did this group solve the problem?

transcript.chinese: 答案：平均多花了三分半钟。
transcript.english: Answer: It took them, on average, three and a half minutes longer.

transcript.chinese: 多了 3.5 分钟。
transcript.english: 3.5 min longer.

transcript.chinese: 这没有意义，对吧？
transcript.english: This makes no sense, right?

transcript.chinese: 我的意思是，我是美国人。
transcript.english: I mean, I'm an American.

transcript.chinese: 我相信自由市场。
transcript.english: I believe in free markets.

transcript.chinese: 这不是它应该如何工作的，对吧？
transcript.english: That's not how it's supposed to work, right?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）如果你希望人们表现得更好，你就奖励他们。
transcript.english: (Laughter) If you want people to perform better, you reward them.

transcript.chinese: 正确的？
transcript.english: Right?

transcript.chinese: 奖金、佣金、他们自己的真人秀。
transcript.english: Bonuses, commissions, their own reality show.

transcript.chinese: 激励他们。
transcript.english: Incentivize them.

transcript.chinese: 商业就是这样运作的。
transcript.english: That's how business works.

transcript.chinese: 但这里并没有发生这种情况。
transcript.english: But that's not happening here.

transcript.chinese: 你有一个旨在提高思维和加速创造力的激励措施，但它的作用恰恰相反。
transcript.english: You've got an incentive designed to sharpen thinking and accelerate creativity, and it does just the opposite.

transcript.chinese: 它使思维迟钝并阻碍创造力。
transcript.english: It dulls thinking and blocks creativity.

transcript.chinese: 这个实验的有趣之处在于它不是一个畸变。
transcript.english: What's interesting about this experiment is that it's not an aberration.

transcript.chinese: 近40年来，这一点被一再复制。
transcript.english: This has been replicated over and over again for nearly 40 years.

transcript.chinese: 这些偶然的激励因素——如果你这样做，那么你就会得到那个——在某些情况下会起作用。
transcript.english: These contingent motivators -- if you do this, then you get that -- work in some circumstances.

transcript.chinese: 但对于很多任务来说，它们实际上要么不起作用，要么常常有害。
transcript.english: But for a lot of tasks, they actually either don't work or, often, they do harm.

transcript.chinese: 这是社会科学中最有力的发现之一，
transcript.english: This is one of the most robust findings in social science,

transcript.chinese: 也是最被忽视的之一。
transcript.english: and also one of the most ignored.

transcript.chinese: 过去几年我一直在研究人类动机的科学，特别是外在动机和内在动机的动态。
transcript.english: I spent the last couple of years looking at the science of human motivation, particularly the dynamics of extrinsic motivators and intrinsic motivators.

transcript.chinese: 我告诉你，这还差得很远。
transcript.english: And I'm telling you, it's not even close.

transcript.chinese: 如果你审视科学，就会发现科学知识与商业行为之间存在不匹配。
transcript.english: If you look at the science, there is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does.

transcript.chinese: 这里令人担忧的是我们的业务操作系统——想想我们业务背后的一系列假设和协议，我们如何激励人们，我们如何应用我们的人力资源——
transcript.english: What's alarming here is that our business operating system -- think of the set of assumptions and protocols beneath our businesses, how we motivate people, how we apply our human resources--

transcript.chinese: 它完全是围绕这些外在激励因素、胡萝卜加大棒建立起来的。
transcript.english: it's built entirely around these extrinsic motivators, around carrots and sticks.

transcript.chinese: 对于 20 世纪的许多任务来说，这实际上已经足够了。
transcript.english: That's actually fine for many kinds of 20th century tasks.

transcript.chinese: 但对于 21 世纪的任务来说，这种机械性的奖惩方法不起作用，而且常常不起作用，而且常常有害。
transcript.english: But for 21st century tasks, that mechanistic, reward-and-punishment approach doesn't work, often doesn't work, and often does harm.

transcript.chinese: 让我告诉你。
transcript.english: Let me show you.

transcript.chinese: 格鲁克斯伯格做了另一个类似的实验，他以稍微不同的方式提出了这个问题，就像这里的这样。
transcript.english: Glucksberg did another similar experiment, he presented the problem in a slightly different way, like this up here.

transcript.chinese: 将蜡烛固定在墙上，这样蜡就不会滴到桌子上。
transcript.english: Attach the candle to the wall so the wax doesn't drip onto the table.

transcript.chinese: 同样的交易。
transcript.english: Same deal.

transcript.chinese: 你：我们正在制定规范。
transcript.english: You: we're timing for norms.

transcript.chinese: 你：我们正在激励。
transcript.english: You: we're incentivizing.

transcript.chinese: 这次发生了什么？
transcript.english: What happened this time?

transcript.chinese: 这一次，受到激励的小组打败了另一组。
transcript.english: This time, the incentivized group kicked the other group's butt.

transcript.chinese: 为什么？
transcript.english: Why?

transcript.chinese: 因为当图钉开箱即用时，这很容易，不是吗？
transcript.english: Because when the tacks are out of the box, it's pretty easy isn't it?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）如果-那么奖励对于这类任务非常有效，因为有一套简单的规则和明确的目的地。
transcript.english: (Laughter) If-then rewards work really well for those sorts of tasks, where there is a simple set of rules and a clear destination to go to.

transcript.chinese: 奖励，就其本质而言，可以缩小我们的注意力，集中注意力；这就是为什么它们在很多情况下都有效。
transcript.english: Rewards, by their very nature, narrow our focus, concentrate the mind; that's why they work in so many cases.

transcript.chinese: 因此，对于像这样的任务，一个狭窄的焦点，你只看到目标就在那里，直接向前推进，它们非常有效。
transcript.english: So, for tasks like this, a narrow focus, where you just see the goal right there, zoom straight ahead to it, they work really well.

transcript.chinese: 但对于真正的蜡烛问题，你不希望看起来像这样。
transcript.english: But for the real candle problem, you don't want to be looking like this.

transcript.chinese: 解决方案就在外围。
transcript.english: The solution is on the periphery.

transcript.chinese: 你想要四处看看。
transcript.english: You want to be looking around.

transcript.chinese: 这种奖励实际上缩小了我们的注意力并限制了我们的可能性。
transcript.english: That reward actually narrows our focus and restricts our possibility.

transcript.chinese: 让我告诉你为什么这如此重要。
transcript.english: Let me tell you why this is so important.

transcript.chinese: 在西欧、亚洲很多地方、北美、澳大利亚，白领做这种工作越来越少，越来越多。
transcript.english: In western Europe, in many parts of Asia, in North America, in Australia, white-collar workers are doing less of this kind of work, and more of this kind of work.

transcript.chinese: 这种常规的、基于规则的左脑工作——某些类型的会计、财务分析、计算机编程——已经变得相当容易外包，相当容易实现自动化。
transcript.english: That routine, rule-based, left-brain work -- certain kinds of accounting, financial analysis, computer programming -- has become fairly easy to outsource, fairly easy to automate.

transcript.chinese: 软件可以更快地做到这一点。
transcript.english: Software can do it faster.

transcript.chinese: 低成本提供商可以更便宜地做到这一点。
transcript.english: Low-cost providers can do it cheaper.

transcript.chinese: 因此，真正重要的是右脑创造性、概念性的能力。
transcript.english: So what really matters are the more right-brained creative, conceptual kinds of abilities.

transcript.chinese: 想想你自己的工作。
transcript.english: Think about your own work.

transcript.chinese: 想想你自己的工作。
transcript.english: Think about your own work.

transcript.chinese: 您面临的问题，甚至我们在这里讨论的问题，是否有一套明确的规则和单一的解决方案？
transcript.english: Are the problems that you face, or even the problems we've been talking about here, do they have a clear set of rules, and a single solution?

transcript.chinese: 号
transcript.english: No.

transcript.chinese: 规则很神秘。
transcript.english: The rules are mystifying.

transcript.chinese: 如果这个解决方案确实存在的话，那也是令人惊讶的，而且并不明显。
transcript.english: The solution, if it exists at all, is surprising and not obvious.

transcript.chinese: 这个房间里的每个人都在处理他们自己版本的蜡烛问题。
transcript.english: Everybody in this room is dealing with their own version of the candle problem.

transcript.chinese: 对于任何领域的任何类型的蜡烛问题，那些“如果-那么”奖励，以及我们围绕其建立的许多业务的东西，都不起作用！
transcript.english: And for candle problems of any kind, in any field, those if-then rewards, the things around which we've built so many of our businesses, don't work!

transcript.chinese: 这让我抓狂。
transcript.english: It makes me crazy.

transcript.chinese: 事情是这样的。
transcript.english: And here's the thing.

transcript.chinese: 这不是一种感觉。
transcript.english: This is not a feeling.

transcript.chinese: 好的？
transcript.english: Okay?

transcript.chinese: 我是一名律师；我不相信感情。
transcript.english: I'm a lawyer; I don't believe in feelings.

transcript.chinese: 这不是一种哲学。
transcript.english: This is not a philosophy.

transcript.chinese: 我是美国人；我不相信哲学。
transcript.english: I'm an American; I don't believe in philosophy.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）这是一个事实——或者，正如我们在我的家乡华盛顿特区所说的那样，这是一个真实的事实。
transcript.english: (Laughter) This is a fact -- or, as we say in my hometown of Washington, D.C., a true fact.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）（鼓掌）我举个例子。
transcript.english: (Laughter) (Applause) Let me give you an example.

transcript.chinese: 让我整理一下这里的证据。
transcript.english: Let me marshal the evidence here.

transcript.chinese: 我不是在讲故事，我是在讲一个案例。
transcript.english: I'm not telling a story, I'm making a case.

transcript.chinese: 陪审团的女士们、先生们，请提供一些证据：丹·艾瑞利 (Dan Ariely)，我们这个时代最伟大的经济学家之一，他和三位同事对麻省理工学院的一些学生进行了一项研究。
transcript.english: Ladies and gentlemen of the jury, some evidence: Dan Ariely, one of the great economists of our time, he and three colleagues did a study of some MIT students.

transcript.chinese: 他们给这些麻省理工学院的学生提供了一堆游戏，这些游戏涉及创造力、运动技能和注意力。
transcript.english: They gave these MIT students a bunch of games, games that involved creativity, and motor skills, and concentration.

transcript.chinese: 根据绩效，他们提供了三个级别的奖励：小奖励、中奖励、大奖励。
transcript.english: And the offered them, for performance, three levels of rewards: small reward, medium reward, large reward.

transcript.chinese: 如果你做得很好，你会得到丰厚的奖励。
transcript.english: If you do really well you get the large reward, on down.

transcript.chinese: 发生了什么？
transcript.english: What happened?

transcript.chinese: 只要任务只涉及机械技能奖金，就会发挥预期的作用：工资越高，表现越好。
transcript.english: As long as the task involved only mechanical skill bonuses worked as they would be expected: the higher the pay, the better the performance.

transcript.chinese: 好的？
transcript.english: Okay?

transcript.chinese: 但一旦任务需要基本的认知技能，更大的奖励就会导致表现更差。
transcript.english: But once the task called for even rudimentary cognitive skill, a larger reward led to poorer performance.

transcript.chinese: 然后他们说，“让我们看看这里是否有任何文化偏见。
transcript.english: Then they said, "Let's see if there's any cultural bias here.

transcript.chinese: 让我们去印度马杜赖测试一下。”生活水平较低。
transcript.english: Let's go to Madurai, India and test it." Standard of living is lower.

transcript.chinese: 在马杜赖，按照北美标准来说适度的奖励在那里更有意义。
transcript.english: In Madurai, a reward that is modest in North American standards, is more meaningful there.

transcript.chinese: 同样的交易。
transcript.english: Same deal.

transcript.chinese: 一堆游戏，三个级别的奖励。
transcript.english: A bunch of games, three levels of rewards.

transcript.chinese: 会发生什么？
transcript.english: What happens?

transcript.chinese: 提供中等奖励的人并不比提供小奖励的人做得更好。
transcript.english: People offered the medium level of rewards did no better than people offered the small rewards.

transcript.chinese: 但这一次，人们提供了最高的奖励，他们却做得最差。
transcript.english: But this time, people offered the highest rewards, they did the worst of all.

transcript.chinese: 在我们通过三个实验检查的九项任务中的八项中，更高的激励导致了更差的表现。
transcript.english: In eight of the nine tasks we examined across three experiments, higher incentives led to worse performance.

transcript.chinese: 这是某种敏感的社会主义阴谋吗？
transcript.english: Is this some kind of touchy-feely socialist conspiracy going on here?

transcript.chinese: 不，这些是来自麻省理工学院、卡内基梅隆大学、芝加哥大学的经济学家。
transcript.english: No, these are economists from MIT, from Carnegie Mellon, from the University of Chicago.

transcript.chinese: 你知道谁赞助了这项研究吗？
transcript.english: Do you know who sponsored this research?

transcript.chinese: 美国联邦储备银行。
transcript.english: The Federal Reserve Bank of the United States.

transcript.chinese: 这就是美国的经验。
transcript.english: That's the American experience.

transcript.chinese: 让我们跨越大洋，来到伦敦政治经济学院（LSE），伦敦政治经济学院，十一位诺贝尔经济学奖获得者的母校。
transcript.english: Let's go across the pond to the London School of Economics, LSE, London School of Economics, alma mater of eleven Nobel Laureates in economics.

transcript.chinese: 乔治·索罗斯、弗里德里希·哈耶克和米克·贾格尔等伟大经济思想家的培训基地。
transcript.english: Training ground for great economic thinkers like George Soros, and Friedrich Hayek, and Mick Jagger.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）上个月，就在上个月，伦敦经济学院的经济学家研究了 51 项关于公司内部绩效工资计划的研究。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Last month, just last month, economists at LSE looked at 51 studies of pay-for-performance plans, inside of companies.

transcript.chinese: 他们是这样说的：“我们发现财务激励可能会对整体绩效产生负面影响。”
transcript.english: Here's what they said: "We find that financial incentives can result in a negative impact on overall performance."

transcript.chinese: 科学知识与商业行为之间存在不匹配。
transcript.english: There is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does.

transcript.chinese: 当我们站在经济崩溃的废墟中时，令我担心的是，太多的组织正在根据过时的、未经检验的、更多地植根于民间传说而不是科学的假设来制定有关人才和人员的决策和政策。
transcript.english: And what worries me, as we stand here in the rubble of the economic collapse, is that too many organizations are making their decisions, their policies about talent and people, based on assumptions that are outdated, unexamined, and rooted more in folklore than in science.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们真的想摆脱这场经济混乱，如果我们真的想在 21 世纪的那些定义性任务上取得高绩效，解决方案不是做更多错误的事情，用更甜的胡萝卜诱惑人们，或者用更锋利的棍子威胁他们。
transcript.english: And if we really want to get out of this economic mess, if we really want high performance on those definitional tasks of the 21st century, the solution is not to do more of the wrong things, to entice people with a sweeter carrot, or threaten them with a sharper stick.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要一种全新的方法。
transcript.english: We need a whole new approach.

transcript.chinese: 好消息是，一直在研究动机的科学家们为我们提供了这种新方法。
transcript.english: The good news is that the scientists who've been studying motivation have given us this new approach.

transcript.chinese: 它更多地围绕内在动机而建立。
transcript.english: It's built much more around intrinsic motivation.

transcript.chinese: 围绕着做事情的愿望，因为它们很重要，因为我们喜欢它，它们很有趣，或者是重要事物的一部分。
transcript.english: Around the desire to do things because they matter, because we like it, they're interesting, or part of something important.

transcript.chinese: 在我看来，我们企业的新操作系统围绕三个要素：自主、掌握和目标。
transcript.english: And to my mind, that new operating system for our businesses revolves around three elements: autonomy, mastery and purpose.

transcript.chinese: 自主：指导我们自己生活的冲动。
transcript.english: Autonomy: the urge to direct our own lives.

transcript.chinese: 精通：渴望在重要的事情上变得越来越好。
transcript.english: Mastery: the desire to get better and better at something that matters.

transcript.chinese: 目的：渴望为比我们自己更伟大的事物服务。
transcript.english: Purpose: the yearning to do what we do in the service of something larger than ourselves.

transcript.chinese: 这些是我们企业全新操作系统的构建模块。
transcript.english: These are the building blocks of an entirely new operating system for our businesses.

transcript.chinese: 我今天只想谈谈自治。
transcript.english: I want to talk today only about autonomy.

transcript.chinese: 在20世纪，我们提出了这种管理理念。
transcript.english: In the 20th century, we came up with this idea of management.

transcript.chinese: 管理并非源于自然。
transcript.english: Management did not emanate from nature.

transcript.chinese: 管理不是一棵树，而是一台电视机。
transcript.english: Management is not a tree, it's a television set.

transcript.chinese: 有人发明了它。
transcript.english: Somebody invented it.

transcript.chinese: 这并不意味着它会永远有效。
transcript.english: It doesn't mean it's going to work forever.

transcript.chinese: 管理很棒。
transcript.english: Management is great.

transcript.chinese: 如果你想要合规性，传统的管理理念是很好的选择。
transcript.english: Traditional notions of management are great if you want compliance.

transcript.chinese: 但如果你想要参与，自我指导效果更好。
transcript.english: But if you want engagement, self-direction works better.

transcript.chinese: 一些自我指导的激进概念的例子。
transcript.english: Some examples of some kind of radical notions of self-direction.

transcript.chinese: 你看不到很多，但你会看到一些真正有趣的事情正在发生，这意味着给人们足够的、公平的、绝对的报酬——把金钱问题排除在外，然后给人们很大的自主权。
transcript.english: You don't see a lot of it, but you see the first stirrings of something really interesting going on, what it means is paying people adequately and fairly, absolutely -- getting the issue of money off the table, and then giving people lots of autonomy.

transcript.chinese: 一些例子。
transcript.english: Some examples.

transcript.chinese: 你们中有多少人听说过 Atlassian 公司？
transcript.english: How many of you have heard of the company Atlassian?

transcript.chinese: 看起来还不到一半。
transcript.english: It looks like less than half.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） Atlassian 是一家澳大利亚软件公司。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Atlassian is an Australian software company.

transcript.chinese: 他们做了一些非常酷的事情。
transcript.english: And they do something incredibly cool.

transcript.chinese: 每年他们都会告诉工程师几次，“在接下来的 24 小时里，做任何你想做的事情，只要它不是你日常工作的一部分。
transcript.english: A few times a year they tell their engineers, "Go for the next 24 hours and work on anything you want, as long as it's not part of your regular job.

transcript.chinese: 做你想做的任何事情。”工程师利用这段时间来开发一个很酷的代码补丁，想出一个优雅的技巧。
transcript.english: Work on anything you want." Engineers use this time to come up with a cool patch for code, come up with an elegant hack.

transcript.chinese: 然后，他们在一天结束时举行的疯狂全体员工会议上，向队友和公司其他成员展示自己开发的所有产品。
transcript.english: Then they present all of the stuff that they've developed to their teammates, to the rest of the company, in this wild and woolly all-hands meeting at the end of the day.

transcript.chinese: 作为澳大利亚人，每个人都喝啤酒。
transcript.english: Being Australians, everybody has a beer.

transcript.chinese: 他们称之为联邦快递日。
transcript.english: They call them FedEx Days.

transcript.chinese: 为什么？
transcript.english: Why?

transcript.chinese: 因为你必须连夜交付一些东西。
transcript.english: Because you have to deliver something overnight.

transcript.chinese: 很漂亮；不错。
transcript.english: It's pretty; not bad.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个巨大的商标侵权行为，但它非常聪明。
transcript.english: It's a huge trademark violation, but it's pretty clever.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）有一天，高度自治已经产生了一系列可能从未存在过的软件修复。
transcript.english: (Laughter) That one day of intense autonomy has produced a whole array of software fixes that might never have existed.

transcript.chinese: 它的效果非常好，以至于 Atlassian 以 20% 的时间将其提升到了一个新的水平——众所周知，谷歌就是这样做的——工程师可以用 20% 的时间做他们想做的任何事情。
transcript.english: It's worked so well that Atlassian has taken it to the next level with 20% time -- done, famously, at Google -- where engineers can spend 20% of their time working on anything they want.

transcript.chinese: 他们对自己的时间、任务、团队和技术拥有自主权。
transcript.english: They have autonomy over their time, their task, their team, their technique.

transcript.chinese: 极大的自主权。
transcript.english: Radical amounts of autonomy.

transcript.chinese: 正如很多人所知，在 Google，每年大约有一半的新产品是在这 20% 的时间内诞生的：例如 Gmail、Orkut、Google News。
transcript.english: And at Google, as many of you know, about half of the new products in a typical year are birthed during that 20% time: things like Gmail, Orkut, Google News.

transcript.chinese: 让我给你举一个更激进的例子：由两位美国顾问创建的名为“只注重结果的工作环境”（ROWE）的东西，在北美各地的十几家公司中得到应用。
transcript.english: Let me give you an even more radical example of it: something called the Results Only Work Environment (the ROWE), created by two American consultants, in place at a dozen companies around North America.

transcript.chinese: 在 ROWE 中，人们没有日程安排。
transcript.english: In a ROWE people don't have schedules.

transcript.chinese: 他们会在需要的时候出现。
transcript.english: They show up when they want.

transcript.chinese: 他们不必在特定时间或任何时间出现在办公室。
transcript.english: They don't have to be in the office at a certain time, or any time.

transcript.chinese: 他们只需要完成他们的工作。
transcript.english: They just have to get their work done.

transcript.chinese: 他们如何做、何时做、在哪里做，完全取决于他们。
transcript.english: How they do it, when they do it, where they do it, is totally up to them.

transcript.chinese: 在此类环境中召开会议是可选的。
transcript.english: Meetings in these kinds of environments are optional.

transcript.chinese: 会发生什么？
transcript.english: What happens?

transcript.chinese: 几乎所有方面，生产率都提高了，员工敬业度提高了，员工满意度提高了，人员流动率下降了。
transcript.english: Almost across the board, productivity goes up, worker engagement goes up, worker satisfaction goes up, turnover goes down.

transcript.chinese: 自主、掌握和目标，是新的做事方式的基石。
transcript.english: Autonomy, mastery and purpose, the building blocks of a new way of doing things.

transcript.chinese: 你们中的一些人可能会看到这个并说：“嗯，这听起来不错，但这是乌托邦。”我说：“不。
transcript.english: Some of you might look at this and say, "Hmm, that sounds nice, but it's Utopian." And I say, "Nope.

transcript.chinese: 我有证据。” 20 世纪 90 年代中期，微软推出了一本名为 Encarta 的百科全书。
transcript.english: I have proof." The mid-1990s, Microsoft started an encyclopedia called Encarta.

transcript.chinese: 他们采取了所有正确的激励措施，他们聘请专业人员撰写和编辑数千篇文章。
transcript.english: They had deployed all the right incentives, They paid professionals to write and edit thousands of articles.

transcript.chinese: 薪酬丰厚的经理负责监督整个过程，以确保项目按时按预算完成。
transcript.english: Well-compensated managers oversaw the whole thing to make sure it came in on budget and on time.

transcript.chinese: 几年后，另一部百科全书开始出版。
transcript.english: A few years later, another encyclopedia got started.

transcript.chinese: 不同型号吧？
transcript.english: Different model, right?

transcript.chinese: 这样做是为了好玩。
transcript.english: Do it for fun.

transcript.chinese: 没有人得到一分钱、一欧元或一日元的报酬。
transcript.english: No one gets paid a cent, or a euro or a yen.

transcript.chinese: 做因为你喜欢做。
transcript.english: Do it because you like to do it.

transcript.chinese: 就在十年前，如果你去找任何地方的经济学家，“嘿，我有这两种不同的模型来创建百科全书。
transcript.english: Just 10 years ago, if you had gone to an economist, anywhere, "Hey, I've got these two different models for creating an encyclopedia.

transcript.chinese: 如果他们正面交锋，谁会赢？”十年前，你在地球上找不到任何一个清醒的经济学家能够预测维基百科模型。
transcript.english: If they went head to head, who would win?" 10 years ago you could not have found a single sober economist anywhere on planet Earth who would have predicted the Wikipedia model.

transcript.chinese: 这是这两种方法之间的激烈战斗。
transcript.english: This is the titanic battle between these two approaches.

transcript.chinese: 这就是阿里-弗雷泽的动机，对吧？
transcript.english: This is the Ali-Frazier of motivation, right?

transcript.chinese: 这就是马尼拉的惊险。
transcript.english: This is the Thrilla in Manila.

transcript.chinese: 内在激励因素与外在激励因素。
transcript.english: Intrinsic motivators versus extrinsic motivators.

transcript.chinese: 自主、掌握和目标，与胡萝卜加大棒相比，谁会获胜？
transcript.english: Autonomy, mastery and purpose, versus carrot and sticks, and who wins?

transcript.chinese: 淘汰赛中的内在动机、自主性、掌控力和目标。
transcript.english: Intrinsic motivation, autonomy, mastery and purpose, in a knockout.

transcript.chinese: 让我总结一下。
transcript.english: Let me wrap up.

transcript.chinese: 科学知识与商业行为之间存在不匹配。
transcript.english: There is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does.

transcript.chinese: 这是科学所知道的。
transcript.english: Here is what science knows.

transcript.chinese: 一：那些 20 世纪的奖励，那些我们认为是商业自然组成部分的激励因素，确实有效，但只在非常有限的情况下有效。
transcript.english: One: Those 20th century rewards, those motivators we think are a natural part of business, do work, but only in a surprisingly narrow band of circumstances.

transcript.chinese: 第二：那些“如果-那么”奖励往往会破坏创造力。
transcript.english: Two: Those if-then rewards often destroy creativity.

transcript.chinese: 三：高绩效的秘诀不是奖励和惩罚，而是看不见的内在动力——为了自己的利益而做事的动力。
transcript.english: Three: The secret to high performance isn't rewards and punishments, but that unseen intrinsic drive-- the drive to do things for their own sake.

transcript.chinese: 做事的动力，因为它们很重要。
transcript.english: The drive to do things cause they matter.

transcript.chinese: 这是最好的部分。
transcript.english: And here's the best part.

transcript.chinese: 我们已经知道这一点了。
transcript.english: We already know this.

transcript.chinese: 科学证实了我们心中所知道的事情。
transcript.english: The science confirms what we know in our hearts.

transcript.chinese: 所以，如果我们修复科学与商业之间的这种不匹配，如果我们将我们的动机、动机的概念带入21世纪，如果我们克服这种懒惰、危险、胡萝卜加大棒的意识形态，我们就可以加强我们的业务，我们就可以解决很多蜡烛问题，也许，也许-
transcript.english: So, if we repair this mismatch between science and business, if we bring our motivation, notions of motivation into the 21st century, if we get past this lazy, dangerous, ideology of carrots and sticks, we can strengthen our businesses, we can solve a lot of those candle problems, and maybe, maybe --

transcript.chinese: 我们可以改变世界。
transcript.english: we can change the world.

transcript.chinese: 我休息一下。
transcript.english: I rest my case.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
